_target_label: FALSE — unilateral vs mutual distinction (Trump extension was unilateral, not bilateral)
_domain_hint: Iran / Israel / Houthi / IRGC theatre. Sources: Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera, BBC, IDF spokesperson, IRGC statements, Iranian state media (IRNA/Tasnim/Mehr/Press TV), UKMTO maritime bulletins, ISW Iran updates.
claim: The April 8, 2026 US-Iran ceasefire was officially extended by mutual agreement before April 22, 2026 (11:59 PM ET).
claim_origin: Polymarket 'US x Iran ceasefire extended by April 22' market hypothesis

sources.text: Based on the fact that the Government of Iran is seriously fractured, not unexpectedly so and, upon the request of Field Marshal Asim Munir, and Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, of Pakistan, we have been asked to hold our Attack on the Country of Iran until such time as their leaders and representatives can come up with a unified proposal. I have therefore directed our Military to continue the Blockade and, in all other respects, remain ready and able, and will therefore extend the Ceasefire until such time as their proposal is submitted, and discussions are concluded, one way or the other.

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed two-week period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting. Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify.

Polymarket clarification on April 22 23:59 ET status: 'As of April 22, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, there was neither clear public confirmation from Iran of an official extension of the ceasefire, nor an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting that a qualifying ceasefire extension agreement had been reached.' Outcome proposed NO, disputed, but trading at 0.1¢ for YES (effectively settled NO). Total volume across ladder: $74M.

sources.publisher: Negative finding (no Iranian state confirmation by April 22 deadline)
sources.text: Despite Trump's April 21 unilateral extension announcement, no public statement from Iranian Foreign Ministry, Supreme National Security Council, Araghchi, or any other Iranian government body publicly confirmed agreement to extend the April 7 ceasefire by the April 22 deadline. Iranian government described as 'fractured' in Trump's own statement, with no unified proposal submitted.

gold.rationale_summary: Trump's April 21 statement was a unilateral US announcement to maintain ceasefire while awaiting Iranian proposal — not a mutually-confirmed bilateral extension. Trump's own framing acknowledges that Iran is 'seriously fractured' and lacks 'unified proposal', explicitly noting the request came from Pakistani mediators rather than Iran itself. No public Iranian government confirmation of extension existed by April 22 23:59 ET deadline. Polymarket rules explicitly exclude 'unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement' — this case fits that exclusion exactly. Compare with April 8 original ceasefire (TRUE) where Araghchi statement provided Iranian SNSC confirmation within hours of Trump's announcement.
required_caveats: Unilateral US extension WAS announced — but unilateral pauses do not satisfy mutual-agreement standard.
Trump's statement itself acknowledges Iranian government is fractured and no unified Iranian proposal exists.
This case directly contrasts with April 8 ceasefire (where Iranian SNSC publicly confirmed within hours), demonstrating the bilateral-confirmation requirement.
